title: Split Plan
description: Full blueprint for splitting a spec when scope-assessment recommends it. Produced by propose-split skill. Consumed by validate-spec Phase 4 reporting and (future) by build-spec materialisation.

applicable.description: False when verdict was not needs_split — the rest of the document is then absent.

reason.description: Present only when applicable=false. The verdict that caused the early exit.

split_rationale.description: 3–5 sentences explaining why this split — references shared signals for cohesion-driven, extraction heuristics for size-driven.

parent_residue.description: Cohesion-driven splits only. The parent feature converts to an index; this captures what stays at the parent level.

connects_to_kept.description: Connects To rows that remain at the parent level (parent-wide upstream/downstream).

subtasks_table.description: Markdown rows for the parent's new ## Subtasks section.

spec_placeholder.description: e.g. 'SPEC-NNN-1' — actual numbers assigned at materialisation

candidate_subtasks.description: Per-candidate full plan when driver=cohesion; thin extraction proposals when driver=size.

kind.description: cohesion = full subtask split; extraction = thin proposal for size-driven case

description.description: 2–4 sentences. Required for kind=cohesion; optional for kind=extraction.

id.description: e.g. US-1 (renumbered for the new subtask)

acs.description: AC text, quoted verbatim from the parent.

technical_requirements.description: TR bullets that move with this candidate. Each item carries its provenance from the parent for audit.

source.description: Provenance — verbatim parent TR bullet, or 'parent TR bullet N (split)' when partitioned

is_sibling.description: True when target is another candidate in this plan

definition_of_done.description: DoD items that close on this candidate's deliverable.

blocked_by.description: Sibling candidate titles whose output this one consumes.

rationale.description: Why this candidate exists — for kind=extraction, the heuristic that surfaced it.

dependency_order.description: Topologically ordered candidate titles. Order in which the candidates should be materialised and implemented.
